metadata.scheme_name: State Scholarship for Class 6th to 10th (Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic )

raw_data.details: The "State Scholarship for Classes 6th to 10th" scheme by the Department of De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Welfare, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides an annual scholarship ranging from ₹200 to ₹1,200 (for 10 months) to the students belonging to De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities studying in classes 6th to 10th. The scholarship is distributed by the Directorate of Public Distribution, Government of Madhya Pradesh.
raw_data.benefits: Annual Scholarship (for 10 months):For Classes 6th to 8thBoys: ₹200Girls: ₹600For Classes 9th to 10thBoys: ₹600Girls: ₹1200
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a student. The applicant should be from a Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Community. The applicant should be studying in Class 6th to 10th.*The applicant should be eligible for the scholarship even if they have failed one year.*The applicant should not need to reapply for renewal if they continue in the same category of classes (e.g., 6th to 8th or 9th to 10th).
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Navigate to the top right corner of the homepage and click "[Registration".](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Registration/Default)
Step 3: On the registration page, provide the mandatory details. (the fields marked with * are mandatory). If required, verify your Mobile Number and/or Email ID via OTP. Finally, click "Register". Application
Step 1: Visit the "[Samarth Portal](https://samarth.mp.gov.in/Default)".
Step 2: Use the credentials you created during registration to log in.
Step 3: Navigate to the online application form of the scheme you intend to apply for.
Step 4: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size.
Step 5: Carefully review all the information provided. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 6: Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any).
Step 7: Click the "Submit" to submit your application.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste CertificateSamagra IDBank Account DetailsProof of Enrolment into Class 6th to 10th
raw_data.scheme_name: State Scholarship for Class 6th to 10th (Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic )

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities are eligible.

faqs.question: What is the scholarship amount for boys in classes 6 to 8?
faqs.answer: Boys in classes 6 to 8 receive an annual scholarship of ₹200.

faqs.question: Is an income certificate required for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, an income certificate is not required for this scholarship.

faqs.question: Do students need to reapply every year?
faqs.answer: No, students do not need to reapply for renewal if they are in the same category of classes (e.g., 6th to 8th or 9th to 10th).

faqs.question: Can a student receive the scholarship if they have failed a year?
faqs.answer: Yes, students are eligible for the scholarship even if they have failed a year.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: An attested photocopy of the Permanent Caste Certificate issued by the Sub-Divisional Officer (Revenue) is required.

faqs.question: How long is the scholarship provided for each year?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is provided for 10 months each academic year.
